title: Central Asia / Fergana Valley crisis
context: The Fergana Valley — a densely populated agricultural basin shared by Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan — is one of Central Asia's most volatile regions, with roughly 110 kilometers of border still in dispute. The valley depends on the Syr Darya river system, whose upstream-downstream dynamics have repeatedly triggered armed clashes, most severely the September 2022 Kyrgyz-Tajik conflict that killed dozens and displaced well over 100,000 people.
